message:
perf(parser): swap std::collections::HashSet for hashbrown in lexer

The SQL lexer uses a transient HashSet to dedupe parameter numbers
while enforcing MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER. On hot prepare() / compile
paths this set is built and dropped every statement, so the hash
cost is visible in profiles. std::collections::HashSet uses
SipHash-1-3 + RandomState by default; hashbrown::HashSet uses
AHash with significantly lower per-operation cost and faster
insert/contains for small sets of integers — the exact shape here.

No behaviour changes: the set is still only used to check
parameter-number uniqueness, and hashbrown::HashSet exposes the same
contains/insert surface we rely on. Parser Cargo.toml now depends on
the workspace hashbrown pin; Cargo.lock records the resulting
fsqlite-parser → hashbrown 0.14.5 edge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
